data.location_found_nepal: Common in low to upper-mid altitudes (1200-1900 m) of Central and Eastern Nepal; found in Ravongla (Sikkim area), Seijosa Forest area.
data.traditional_usage: Used for the treatment of skin diseases, as fodder for livestock, for the eradication of bed bugs and lice of fowl, and the leaves are used in the preparation of yeast cakes (Marcha).
data.preparation_method: Traditional: Leaf paste applied topically for skin diseases. Laboratory: Crude methanol extraction via maceration of the rhizome.

pathogen_data.preparation_method: Maceration of rhizome to obtain crude methanol extract
pathogen_data.assay_method: Disc diffusion method for ZOI; Broth micro-dilution for MIC/MMC

data.location_found_nepal: Bhojpur, Baglung, Lalitpur, and Kavrepalanchowk districts (Eastern and Central Nepal)
data.traditional_usage: Fresh leaf paste is applied topically to treat cut wounds and skin infections; root and stem decoctions are used for asthma; leaf juice is used for diarrhea.
data.preparation_method: Maceration of dried leaf powder for 72 hours
data.extraction_solvent: Ethanol, Petroleum ether, Ethyl acetate, n-Butanol
data.compound_class: Phenylethanoid glycosides (verbascoside, isoacteoside), Iridoid glycosides, Diterpenoids

data.location_found_nepal: Central, East, and West Nepal; Annapurna Conservation Area (Lwang, Sikles); Makawanpur District.
